verdict_summary: TRPCClientError 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object' STILL fires (elapsedMs=41, fast-fail) — h2_end_stream fix unblocked stream framing but V5-A schema gap remains the actual SPA blocker; warning absent, RRN absent (S2 baseline preserved).

recommendation: next: V5-A schema is INSUFFICIENT despite 786->992 byte rev with 11 added fields. The TRPC client deserialization (likely zod/superjson on response) still hits an undefined property path. Required: (a) capture the FULL legitimate Mistral response payload from a real chat session and diff against current forged response; (b) examine the cause stack to identify which specific field path triggers Object.keys(undefined) or Object.entries(undefined). Likely candidates: nested message.parts[].content shape, attachments[], toolCalls[], or a discriminated-union tag field. h2_end_stream=1 should be KEPT (it correctly closes stream); the framing fix is real and validated by elapsedMs=41 fast-fail vs prior indefinite hang.
notes: Notable: elapsedMs=41 (very fast fail) confirms L-051 h2_end_stream=1 IS effective at closing stream — SPA no longer waits indefinitely. But this only EXPOSES the underlying schema gap rather than masking it as a hang. Net progress: hang -> immediate error. Root cause shifted from 'stream framing' to 'JSON schema mismatch' as previously hypothesized. Same TRPCClientError pattern as prior runs — full match. User session was guest mode (Sign in / Sign up visible) but mutation still processed (Mistral allows guest chat). Console shows: 5 errors total, mutation #5 newChat threw TRPCClientError synchronously upon receiving forged response. cause is TypeError from Object.keys/Object.entries/Object.assign on undefined — typical zod/superjson deserialization failure on an unexpected null/undefined nested field.
